sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: Booking Holdings Inc.의 공시 핵심을 한국어로 요약했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 연간 총예약액은 186.1달러로 12%, 매출은 26.9달러로 13% 성장했으며, FY 조정 EBITDA 마진은 36.9%로 개선됐습니다.
4분기 GAAP 순이익은 1.4달러로 34% 증가했고, 연간 순이익은 5.4달러를 기록했습니다.

riskChanges.text: 순이익 마진이 20.1%로 전년 24.8% 대비 하락했으며, 2026년 전략적 재투자 확대로 단기 마진 압박이 예상됩니다.

businessChanges.text: 경영진은 생성형 AI 활용 확대와 Transformation Program에서 확보한 절감액을 2026년 전략적 재투자에 활용해 장기 성장을 지원할 계획이라고 밝혔습니다.
4분기 총예약액의 불변통화 기준 성장률은 약 11%로, 환율 효과를 제외한 실질 성장이 견조했습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 자사주 매입으로 4분기에 21억 달러, 연간으로는 218억 달러를 집행했습니다.
4분기 조정 EBITDA는 2.2달러, 연간 조정 EBITDA는 9.9달러이며 연간 GAAP EPS는 165.57달러였습니다.
FY 2025 조정 EBITDA 마진은 193포인트 개선됐으며, FY 조정 EPS는 228.06달러를 기록했습니다.

watchItems.text: 2026년 전략적 재투자 확대와 생성형 AI 기반 Connected Trip 실행 가속화 여부가 성장 지속의 핵심 변수입니다.
연간 객실 숙박일수 12억 3,500만 달러 돌파와 마진 회복 추이가 주주가치 제고의 분수령이 될 것입니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: EX-99.1 2 q4-25bkngearningsrelease.htm EX-99.1 q4-25bkngearningsrelease NORWALK, CT - February 18, 2026…Booking Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: BKNG) (the "Company," "we," "our," or "us") today reported its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results. Fourth Quarter 2025 Highlights ● Room nights grew 9% compared to 2024. ● Gross bookings grew 16% compared to 2024, or approximately 11% on a constant currency basis. ● Revenue grew 16% compared to 2024, or approximately 11% on a constant currency basis. ● Enabled approximately $550 million in annual run-rate savings (high end of our prior target) through the Transformation Program,(1) and expect to realize these run-rate savings by the end of 2026. ● In Q1 2026, our Board of Directors approved a 25-to-1 stock split of the Company’s authorized shares of common stock, which will be effected on April 2, 2026. Full Year 2025 Highlights ● Room ni  Normalized numeric aliases: $550 million = 5억 5,000만 달러; $550 = 550달러.

ghts grew 8% compared to 2024. ● Gross bookings grew 12% compared to 2024, or approximately 10% on a constant currency basis. ● Revenue grew 13% compared to 2024, or approximately 10% on a constant currency basis. ● Net income margin was 20.1% (24.8% in 2024). Adjusted EBITDA margin was 36.9% (35.0% in 2024). Q4 2025 FY 2025 (#) (Δ Y/Y) (#) (Δ Y/Y) Room Nights 285M 9% 1,235M 8% Gross Bookings $43.0B 16% $186.1B 12% Revenue $6.3B 16% $26.9B 13% GAAP Net Income $1.4B 34% $5.4B (8%) GAAP EPS $44.22 38% $165.57 (4%) Adjusted EPS $48.80 17% $228.06 22% Adjusted EBITDA $2.2B 19% $9.9B 20% Net Cash Provided By Operating Activities $1.5B 107% $9.4B 13% Free Cash Flow $1.4B 120% $9.1B 15% (1) See note (f) in the Reconci  Normalized numeric aliases: $43.0 = 43.0달러; $186.1 = 186.1달러; $6.3 = 6.3달러; $26.9 = 26.9달러; $1.4 = 1.4달러; $5.4 = 5.4달러; $44.22 = 44.22달러; $165.57 = 165.57달러; $228.06 = 228.06달러; $9.1 = 9.1달러; $2.2 = 2.2달러; $9.9 = 9.9달러; $1.5 = 1.5달러; $9.4 = 9.4달러; 1,235 million = 12억 3,500만 달러.

on of constant currency and non-GAAP financial measures, including Adjusted Net income, Adjusted EPS, Adjusted EBITDA, Adjusted EBITDA margin, Free Cash Flow, and Adjusted Fixed Operating Expenses, and reconciliations to the most directly comparable GAAP measures. Gross bookings is an operating and statistical metric that captures the total dollar value, generally inclusive of taxes and fees, of all travel services booked by our customers, net of cancellations. 1 Booking Holdings Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Financial Results Exhibit 99.1 “We are pleased to report strong results for 2025, delivering double-digit revenue growth, expanding Adjusted EBITDA margin by 193 basis points (Net income margin was 20.1% in 2025 and 24.8% in 2024), and accelerating room night growth in every quarter. These results highlight the strength of our platform and the discipline of our execution,” said Glenn  Normalized numeric aliases: 193 basis points = 193포인트.

Fogel, Chief Executive Officer of Booking Holdings. “We remain focused on our key priorities, especially advancing our use of Generative AI to enhance the value we deliver to both travelers and partners. Savings from the Transformation Program will help us continue to reinvest strategically in the business in 2026 to support sustained, long-term growth." Active Return of Capital to Shareholders First Quarter Dividend ● Our Board of Directors declared a cash dividend of $10.50 per share, payable on March 31, 2026, which is a 9.4% increase from the quarterly cash dividend per share of $9.60 in 2025. The cash dividend will be payable to stockholders of record as of the close of business on March 6, 2026. Stock Repurchase Program ● We repurchased $2.1 billion of stock in the quarter ended December 31, 2025 with a total remaining authorization of $21.8  Normalized numeric aliases: $2.1 billion = 21억 달러; $21.8 billion = 218억 달러; $10.50 = 10.50달러; $9.60 = 9.60달러; $2.1 = 2.1달러; $21.8 = 21.8달러.
